quickStart.title: Quick Start - Recommended Setup

analytics.title: Analytics

analytics.option2: or GTM + GA4 - more features, needs Google account

marketing.title: Meta Conversions

quickStart.result: After completing these steps you'll have: analytics, conversion tracking, and GDPR compliance. Conversions will work even with adblockers.

gtm.title: Do I need Google Tag Manager?
gtm.whenNeededDesc: GTM is the entry point for the Google ecosystem (GA4, Google Ads). If adblockers block GTM, all Google tracking stops working.

scenario1.title: Meta conversions only (no analytics)
scenario1.desc: Simplest setup. Only tracks purchases/leads to Meta. No page view analytics.

scenario2.title: Meta conversions + Umami analytics
scenario2.desc: Privacy-focused analytics. Umami requires hosting (Umami Cloud ~$9/mo or self-hosted). No Google dependency.

scenario3.title: GA4 + Meta (accept ~15-30% loss from adblockers)
scenario3.desc: Standard setup. Adblockers block GA4 for some users, but Meta conversions still work via CAPI.

scenario4.title: GA4 + Meta + bypass adblockers
scenario4.capi: Our CAPI: Optional (GTM Server can send to Meta)
scenario4.desc: Full tracking. All tags load from your domain. Best data quality. Requires GTM Server hosting.

scenario5.title: Google Ads remarketing
scenario5.desc: GTM Server strongly recommended for Google Ads (better cookies, bypass adblockers, improved conversion tracking).

gtm.serverSetup: How to set up GTM Server?

serverOptions.aws: AWS/Azure - for enterprises, requires DevOps knowledge
serverOptions.vps: VPS (e.g., Mikrus, Hetzner) - cheapest (~$5/month), requires manual setup

gtm.serverNote: After setup, you'll get a URL like https://gtm.yourdomain.com - enter it in 'GTM Server URL' field.

facebook.title: How to configure Meta (Facebook) tracking?
facebook.quickStartDesc: Don't know what to configure? Just follow these 4 steps:

quickStartSteps.step1: Go to Meta Events Manager → Your Pixel → Settings
quickStartSteps.step2: Copy Pixel ID → paste in 'Pixel ID' field below
quickStartSteps.step3: Generate Access Token → paste in 'CAPI Token' field

facebook.quickStartResult: Done! Your purchases and leads will be tracked to Meta. Works even with adblockers.
facebook.quickStartOptional: Optional: Go to Consents tab and enable 'Send conversions without cookie consent' to track conversions even when users decline cookies.
facebook.whatIsDesc: Meta Conversions API (CAPI) sends events directly from your server to Meta, bypassing browser limitations. It works alongside the Pixel for better tracking accuracy and ad optimization.
facebook.step1: Go to Meta Events Manager → Data Sources → Your Pixel
facebook.step3: Copy the Pixel ID and paste it above
facebook.step4: Copy the access token and paste it as CAPI Token
facebook.deduplicationDesc: Both Pixel and CAPI send the same event with matching event_id. Meta automatically deduplicates so you don't get double counting.
facebook.recommendationDesc: For sending conversions without cookie consent, we recommend using our built-in CAPI:
facebook.recommendationNote: Use GTM Server only if you need to bypass adblockers for ALL tracking (including Pixel, GA4) or already have GTM Server infrastructure.
facebook.gtmWarning: Have Meta CAPI tag in GTM Server?
facebook.gtmWarningDesc: If your GTM Server container has a Meta Conversions API tag, make sure it uses the event_id field from dataLayer. This way both GTM Server and our CAPI will send the same event_id, and Meta will deduplicate correctly.

consent.title: How does consent management work?
consent.howItWorksDesc: When consent is enabled, users see a cookie banner. Tracking scripts only run after consent is given. This ensures GDPR/ePrivacy compliance.
consent.legitimateInterestDesc: You can track conversions without consent under legitimate interest if tracking is essential for your business and doesn't override user rights. This is a legal gray area - consult your lawyer.
consent.purchaseDesc: Payment completed - essential for order tracking
consent.leadDesc: Free product signup - essential for lead generation
consent.notAllowed: ViewContent, InitiateCheckout, AddPaymentInfo - NOT allowed without consent

embedSnippet.title: Generate embed checkout snippet
embedSnippet.subtitle: Sell this product on any external page. Stripe Embedded for paid, email gate for free.
embedSnippet.step1: Paste snippet anywhere in the HTML of the host page
embedSnippet.step2: Add host page domain to SELLF_EMBED_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
embedSnippet.step3: Open host page — checkout renders automatically
embedSnippet.paidInfoBody: Renders Stripe Embedded Checkout (cards, BLIK, Google Pay). After payment buyer is sent to sellf.app — magic link delivers product access.
embedSnippet.freeInfoBody: Renders an email form with Cloudflare Turnstile. After submit user gets a magic link and product access.
embedSnippet.originsBody: The domain where you paste the snippet MUST be in SELLF_EMBED_ALLOWED_ORIGINS (Sellf env) or seller_embed_settings (DB). Otherwise the browser blocks the request with CORS 403.
embedSnippet.disabledTitle: Embed is disabled for this product
embedSnippet.disabledBody: Go back to product edit (Step 3: Sales) and turn on the “Embed checkout” toggle.
embedSnippet.optionModalHelp: (renders a button instead of inline; checkout opens in an overlay on click)
embedSnippet.optionShowPrice: Show price on the button (e.g. “Buy now · $19.99”)
embedSnippet.modalInfoExtra: Modal mode: button mounts instantly (no network); checkout loads on click. Close with ×, outside-click, or Escape.

loginwallSnippet.title: Generate login wall snippet
loginwallSnippet.subtitle: Gate any page on your own domain — visitors must be signed in and own this product to see the content.
loginwallSnippet.step1: Paste the snippet inside the <head> of the page you want to gate.
loginwallSnippet.step2: Open the page — visitors without access are bounced through sign-in and back.
loginwallSnippet.step3: Returning visitors with active access see the page immediately.
loginwallSnippet.howItWorksBody: A short-lived signed token is added to the URL after sign-in. The snippet checks for it on load, then strips it from the URL with history.replaceState so visitors share clean links.
loginwallSnippet.emptyAllowlistBody: The login wall will reject the redirect with 400 until you add the domain you paste the snippet on to allowed_embed_origins (Settings → Embed) or to the SELLF_EMBED_ALLOWED_ORIGINS environment variable.

deployPaths.title: Pick the path that fits you
deployPaths.subtitle: Three honest options from clone to live shop. Every one tested end-to-end. No marketing magic — what you'll actually click is listed below.
deployPaths.reassurance: Every path above is tested end-to-end — same one-click buttons live in the GitHub README. Pick what fits today; you can move to another later without touching the code.

prereqs.label: Free accounts you'll need on any path
prereqs.stripeSub: Free, ~2 min to sign up at stripe.com

vercel.name: Vercel + Supabase
vercel.tagline: Easiest start. Both on a free tier, both stay free for small shops.
vercel.bestFor: Hobby projects, first store, side gigs, validating an idea
steps: Sign in to Vercel and add the Supabase integration
Paste 5 Stripe + secret values (one-liner commands in guide)
Hit deploy, wait ~3 min for first build
Sign up (first user = admin) → click 'Register webhook' in admin
vercel.footnote: Vercel Hobby is for personal / non-commercial use (rarely enforced for small sites). For commercial shops at scale → VPS path or Vercel Pro ($20/mo).

netlify.name: Netlify + Supabase
netlify.tagline: Same idea on a different host. Netlify free tier + Supabase free tier.
netlify.bestFor: Netlify users, JAMstack-style preferences, alternative if Vercel ToS is a worry
steps: Sign in to Netlify, fork the repo
Create a Supabase project (manual — no integration yet)
Paste 10 env vars in Netlify (full list in guide)
Sign up (first user = admin) → click 'Register webhook' in admin
netlify.footnote: Netlify free tier: 100 GB bandwidth, 300 build minutes / month. Plenty for early-stage shops.

vps.name: Your own VPS
vps.tagline: True self-hosted. Best fit when the shop is your livelihood.
vps.bestFor: Commercial shops, full control, custom domain, no platform ToS to worry about
steps: Plus a VPS (1GB+) and a Cloudflare-managed domain — only the self-host path needs these two
Pick A) edit one env file + 1 curl-bash OR B) 3 OAuth-style curl-bashes (no key pasting)

vercelSlides.title: Click 'Deploy with Vercel'
vercelSlides.caption: GitHub README has a big black 'Deploy' button right at the top. One click takes you straight to Vercel — you don't need to fork anything by hand.

vercelSlides.title: Sign in to Vercel
vercelSlides.caption: First time? Click 'Continue with GitHub' — fastest signup. A free Hobby account is all you need.

vercelSlides.title: Add the Supabase integration
vercelSlides.caption: Vercel asks if you want to connect a database. Click 'Add Integration' → Supabase → free tier. The database creates itself and the keys fill in automatically.

vercelSlides.title: Paste 5 env values
vercelSlides.caption: Stripe publishable + secret keys + 3 random secrets. The QUICK-START guide has copy-paste one-liner commands that generate each secret for you. No webhook secret needed — you'll register it from the admin panel with one click after deploy.

vercelSlides.title: Build runs — coffee break
vercelSlides.caption: About 3 minutes. Default URL is your-shop.vercel.app — sign up (first user = admin), open Settings → Payments, click 'Register webhook'. Done. Optionally add your own yourshop.com in Settings → Domains (free SSL, ~5 min).

netlifySlides.title: Click 'Deploy to Netlify'
netlifySlides.caption: Same README, second button below the Vercel one. Green Netlify badge. Click it.

netlifySlides.title: Sign in to Netlify
netlifySlides.caption: Continue with GitHub. Free tier, no card. Netlify forks the Sellf repo into your GitHub account.

netlifySlides.title: Create a Supabase project
netlifySlides.caption: Netlify doesn't auto-create the database — open supabase.com in another tab, click 'New project', and copy the URL + anon key + service role key.

netlifySlides.title: Paste 10 env vars
netlifySlides.caption: More fields than Vercel (no auto-integration). The guide lists every value with one-liner commands. Paste, save. No webhook secret needed — that comes via the admin click after deploy.

netlifySlides.title: Build runs — coffee break
netlifySlides.caption: About 3 minutes. Default URL is your-shop.netlify.app — sign up (first user = admin), open Settings → Payments, click 'Register webhook'. Done. Optionally add your own yourshop.com in Domain management (free SSL, no card).

vpsSlides.title: VPS path extras — beyond the shared prereqs
vpsSlides.caption: On top of the Stripe + Supabase accounts you'd need for any path, the self-host route adds two things: a 1GB+ VPS (Hetzner ~$5/mo, Mikrus, DigitalOcean — anything works, identical commands) and a domain whose DNS lives in Cloudflare (free zone — you only need an API token).

vpsSlides.title: SSH in + pick a variant
vpsSlides.caption: Two ways to install — both deploy the same thing. Pick whichever you prefer; both shown below.

vpsSlides.title: ~3 min build → live URL
vpsSlides.caption: Streamed log shows Bun + Caddy + PM2 installing, the Cloudflare DNS record going green, Let's Encrypt cert issued, PM2 marking the app online. When the last line reads ✓ Live, you can open https://sellf.example.com in a browser. The first person who signs up automatically becomes the admin.

vpsSlides.title: (Optional) Add Stripe — admin UI, two cards in one page
vpsSlides.caption: The shop already runs without Stripe — catalog, lead magnets and waitlist signups all work out of the box. Wire Stripe in only when you actually want to sell paid products: sign in as admin, open Settings → Payments. Two cards on the same page — paste Stripe pk + sk into the API keys card (saved encrypted to your Supabase DB), then click 'Register webhook' in the second card. Sellf calls Stripe for you and stores the signing secret. Prefer editing files? nano /opt/stacks/sellf-sellf/admin-panel/.env.local + pm2 restart sellf-sellf works too.

items.q: Can I run this without sysadmin skills?
items.a: Yes. We provide a one-line deploy script for any modern VPS (DigitalOcean, Hetzner, Mikrus). The provided guide walks through SSL, backups, and domain pointing step by step. If you can copy-paste a command, you can run Sellf.

items.q: What does it actually cost me per year?
items.a: A VPS that fits Sellf costs $5-15/month (Hetzner CX22, DigitalOcean basic). Stripe charges its normal ~3.4% per transaction. There's no Sellf subscription. Total: roughly $60-180/year of infrastructure regardless of how much you sell.

items.q: What happens if the server goes down?
items.a: Sellf creates automatic backups. If your VPS dies, you spin up a new one and restore from the latest backup — usually under 5 minutes. Your Stripe account and payment data are never lost (Stripe stores them).

items.q: What happens if a webhook fails?
items.a: Failed deliveries land in a dead-letter queue with exponential backoff. You see them in the dashboard and can replay any of them with one click.

items.q: Can I drive Sellf from Claude / an AI agent?
items.a: Yes — Sellf ships an MCP server with 45 tools, 4 resources, and 6 prompts. Add it to Claude Desktop and ask it to list products, refund a payment, or pull analytics.

items.q: Do you handle VAT / Polish JPK / OSS?
items.a: Sellf records the data and exports CSVs. You stay the Merchant of Record so you keep full control (and your data), and you grow into VAT / OSS handling on your own timeline.

objections.title: "Yes, but…" — let's talk about your concerns
objections.subtitle: The fears that stop most people from leaving Gumroad. Here's what actually happens.

items.title: "I've never set up a server"
items.body: One-line deploy. You paste a command, the server installs itself. Detailed guide for SSL, domain, backups. Most users finish in 10 minutes. If you can paste a command, you can run Sellf.

items.title: "What if the server crashes?"
items.body: Automatic backups. New VPS spins up in 5 minutes from the backup. Your Stripe account and payment data stay with Stripe — never lost.

items.title: "How much will this actually cost me?"
items.body: VPS: 25-50 zł/mo (forever). Stripe: ~3.4% per sale (no subscription). Domain: 50 zł/year. Total: under 700 zł/year regardless of revenue. Save thousands vs Gumroad.

items.title: "Is my buyers' data safe?"
items.body: Stripe keys never leave your .env file. Database has row-level security on every table. Sellf passed a security audit, has 2,650 automated tests. Your buyers' data lives on your server, not in someone else's cloud.
